text.title: EF Core Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding production EF Core 8 patterns.

rule.statement: Entity mapping (table names, keys, indexes, max-lengths, default-value SQL, cascade behaviour) MUST live in `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` classes applied via `modelBuilder.ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly(...)`. Data annotations (`[Required]`, `[MaxLength]`) on entities are forbidden.

rationale.source: EF Core docs — Configuring entity types

rule.rationale: Fluent API supports the full feature surface (HasDefaultValueSql, owned types, shadow properties); annotations cover only a subset and split mapping across attribute/class.

rule.statement: All read-only queries MUST use `.AsNoTracking()` (or `.AsNoTrackingWithIdentityResolution()` when navigation identity matters) and project to a DTO/anonymous type before materialization.
rule.rationale: Tracked entities consume change-tracker memory and CPU; projection short-circuits unnecessary entity hydration.

rule.statement: Repository methods MUST NOT return `IQueryable<T>`. They MUST materialize (`ToListAsync`, `FirstOrDefaultAsync`) and return `PagedResult<TDto>` for list queries: `{Items, TotalCount, Page, PageSize}`.
rule.rationale: Returning `IQueryable` leaks query execution outside the persistence boundary — callers can extend the query, breaking encapsulation and forcing DbContext to outlive the repository.

rule.statement: Any query whose `.Include()` chain includes ≥ 2 collection navigations MUST add `.AsSplitQuery()`. Single-collection or scalar-only Include chains MAY keep the default single-query mode.

rationale.source: EF Core docs — Single vs split queries

rule.rationale: Joining over two collection navigations produces a Cartesian explosion (rows × rows). `AsSplitQuery` issues separate SELECTs and merges client-side.

rule.statement: Migrations are append-only. After a migration is merged to the deploy branch, its `*.cs` file MUST NOT be edited. Fixes go in a new migration with a verb-prefixed name (`AlterUserAddIsActive`, `BackfillOrderNumber`).

rationale.source: EF Core docs — Migrations + team workflow

rule.rationale: Editing a deployed migration produces drift between environments; rerun-safety depends on the migration sequence matching `__EFMigrationsHistory` on every database.
